steps.title: Use corresponding side ratios
steps.content: The similarity statement gives the correspondence $A\leftrightarrow X$, $B\leftrightarrow Y$, and $C\leftrightarrow Z$, so $AB$ corresponds to $XY$ and $BC$ corresponds to $YZ$.

steps.title: Identify corresponding sides and set up proportion
steps.content:
Since $A \leftrightarrow X$, $B \leftrightarrow Y$, and $C \leftrightarrow Z$, the sides correspond as: $AB \leftrightarrow XY$ and $BC \leftrightarrow YZ$.
For similar triangles, the ratios of corresponding sides are equal:
$$\frac{AB}{XY} = \frac{BC}{YZ}$$
Substituting the known values:
$$\frac{170}{XY} = \frac{850}{60}$$

steps.title: Cross multiply and solve for XY
steps.content:
Cross multiply to eliminate the fractions:
$$170 \times 60 = 850 \times XY$$
$$10,200 = 850 \times XY$$
Divide both sides by 850:
$$XY = \frac{10,200}{850} = 12$$
That matches Choice D — $12$.
